title: Significant Problem, Public Value, and Durable Need · Claim Audit: Existing Alternative
summary: A novel technical idea lacks evidence of a consequential recurring problem, reachable affected people, existing alternatives, or value beyond demonstration. Predict the decision, affected people, evidence strength, failure cost, and transfer limit before seeing a worked case. Planned artifact: Add and verify the capstone existing alternative increment in significance and public-value case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and eviden…
objectives: Study current services, manual workarounds, nonuse, competitors, public infrastructure, and build-nothing options.
Bound affected populations, contexts, frequency, severity, unequal burden, and who is missing from available evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# capstone-project cumulative independent product evidence portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic evidence-contract review only.
# They never contact participants or services, execute commands, deploy, or claim live conformance.
workspace: portfolio
project-id: capstone-project
stakeholder: [replace with affected people and accountable decision owner]
decision: [replace with the decision this evidence changes]
problem-evidence: [replace with observed need, counterevidence, and uncertainty]
accessibility: [replace with keyboard, structure, reflow, status, alternatives, and human review]
security: [replace with threat, authorization, abuse, response, and residual risk]
privacy: [replace with purpose, minimization, retention, access, correction, and deletion]
test-strategy: [replace with behavior, changed case, fault, accessibility, security, and recovery]
release-evidence: [replace with revision, artifact, inventory, provenance, validation, and rollback]
recovery: [replace with backup, clean restore, reconciliation, communication, and ownership]
portfolio-proof: [replace with contribution, limitations, counterexample, and unseen defense]
transfer-boundary: authorized controlled research, toolchain, deployment, and review environments
evidence:

steps.title: Predict Existing Alternative · Theory Existing Alternative
steps.instruction:
theory case — A novel technical demonstration lacks durable need evidence, reachable affected people, current alternatives, or a causal route to public value. The theory variation 2 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Study current services, manual workarounds, nonuse, competitors, public infrastructure, and build-nothing options.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Existing Alternative contract for the supplied data.

Evidence target: Predict Existing Alternative · Theory Existing Alternative.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for study current services, manual workarounds, nonuse, competitors, public infrastructure, and build-nothing options. For Predict Existing Alternative · Theory Existing Alternative, keep Existing Alternative connected to mechanically reviewable stakeholder, affected-user, artifact, changed-case, failure, repair, release, recovery, and transfer decision evidence.

content.text: A novel technical demonstration lacks durable need evidence, reachable affected people, current alternatives, or a causal route to public value. The theory variation 2 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Study current services, manual workarounds, nonuse, competitors, public infrastructure, and build-nothing options.

content.title: Commit before feedback
content.text: Choose from the stated data before opening the worked model. Record which Existing Alternative condition decided your prediction.

content.label: Authority to consult after committing
content.value: NACE Career Readiness Competencies — Controls reflection and evidence for career and self-development, communication, critical thinking, leadership, professionalism, teamwork, technology, and inclusive practice.

hints: For Predict Existing Alternative · Theory Existing Alternative, name the types, ownership, and stop path, Existing Alternative precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Predict Existing Alternative · Theory Existing Alternative, trace this concrete input and predict values, errors, blocking, and output before reviewing it: A novel technical demonstration lacks durable need evidence, reachable affected people, current alternatives, or a causal route to public value. The theory variation 2 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Study current services, manual workarounds, nonuse, competitors, public infrastructure, and build-nothing options.

For Predict Existing Alternative · Theory Existing Alternative, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: Competitor feature lists explain why people use current alternatives.

options.text: Competitor feature lists explain why people use current alternatives. That claim does not survive the predict existing alternative evidence boundary.
Apply the visible technique immediately, omit assumptions, and treat one successful output as complete proof.
Study current services, manual workarounds, nonuse, competitors, public infrastructure, and build-nothing options. Apply it to the concrete input and state the observable result.

steps.instruction:
theory case — A novel technical demonstration lacks durable need evidence, reachable affected people, current alternatives, or a causal route to public value. content.text: Study current services, manual workarounds, nonuse, competitors, public infrastructure, and build-nothing options. Use this as an observable contract: name its preconditions, trace the state or value change, and state what evidence would disprove the result.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Name the stakeholder decision, affected people, authority, assumption, revision, artifact, observable behavior, changed case, rejected failure, accessibility, security, privacy, operation, recovery, contribution, residual risk, and transfer limit; then calibrate the public claim to that exact evidence.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: A novel technical demonstration lacks durable need evidence, reachable affected people, current alternatives, or a causal route to public value. The worked example variation 2 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Study current services, manual workarounds, nonuse, competitors, public infrastructure, and build-nothing options. A defensible solution applies Existing Alternative, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to significance and public-value case.

content.code:
workspace: portfolio
project-id: changed-product-5
stakeholder: affected-user review panel
decision: continue only if the changed task meets its outcome and guardrails
artifact: revision-bound evidence packet
claim: one bounded behavior survives the changed context
verify: reproduce the task, inspect the artifact, and compare the predeclared measure
changed-case: different access need, input, constraint, and dependency failure
failure: reject plausible output without causal, human, recovery, or ownership evidence
repair: isolate the first unsupported transition, correct it, and add a regression
owner: named learner and stakeholder reviewer
transfer-boundary: authorized controlled research and release environment
content.caption: Runnable transformation example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: Competitor feature lists explain why people use current alternatives. This fails the Existing Alternative contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.

content.label: Research anchor
content.value: NACE Career Readiness Competencies (Current eight-competency framework reviewed 2026-07-15) — Controls reflection and evidence for career and self-development, communication, critical thinking, leadership, professionalism, teamwork, technology, and inclusive practice.

content.label: Mastery evidence
content.value: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Study current services, manual workarounds, nonuse, competitors, public infrastructure, and build-nothing options.

steps.title: Build Existing Alternative evidence · Theory Existing Alternative
steps.instruction:
Append a capstone-existing-alternative-1dcipj: manifest entry with concrete verify plus failure, owner, or repair evidence.

Evidence target: Build Existing Alternative evidence · Theory Existing Alternative.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Existing Alternative into observable performance evidence. For Build Existing Alternative evidence · Theory Existing Alternative, keep Existing Alternative connected to mechanically reviewable stakeholder, affected-user, artifact, changed-case, failure, repair, release, recovery, and transfer decision evidence.

  repair: [replace with a local reproduction and correction path]

steps.title: Defend the Existing Alternative model · Theory Existing Alternative
steps.instruction:
Write the theory evidence note for Existing Alternative: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A novel technical demonstration lacks durable need evidence, reachable affected people, current alternatives, or a causal route to public value. The theory variation 3 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Study current services, manual workarounds, nonuse, competitors, public infrastructure, and build-nothing options.

Evidence target: Defend the Existing Alternative model · Theory Existing Alternative.

For Defend the Existing Alternative model · Theory Existing Alternative, keep Existing Alternative connected to mechanically reviewable stakeholder, affected-user, artifact, changed-case, failure, repair, release, recovery, and transfer decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Study current services, manual workarounds, nonuse, competitors, public infrastructure, and build-nothing options.
